pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 26), export_result.sh (line 9), verifier.py analysis. DrTuxTest is the official MedinTux demo database loaded from SQL dumps bundled with the MedinTux 2.16.012 installer.

Confirmed in export_result.sh line 9: DEMO_FILE="/home/ga/medintux_stored_procedures_demo.txt". This matches the task description which references ~/medintux_stored_procedures_demo.txt (where ~ expands to /home/ga for user 'ga').

Confirmed in setup_task.sh (lines 27-30) which drops these routines, and in verifier.py (lines 56-82) which checks for all 4 routines by name and type. The task description specifies all 4 routines with their exact names and types.

The metadata claims test_search_term is 'a', but export_result.sh line 64 explicitly calls 'CALL sp_search_patients("TESTER")' with the string 'TESTER', and verifier.py line 106 checks for "TESTER" in the output, not "a". The test patient 'TESTER' John is created in setup_task.sh line 42.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates four MySQL stored procedures in the DrTuxTest database, which is the official MedinTux 2.16.012 demo database loaded from SQL dumps bundled with the MedinTux installer (available on SourceForge). The database uses the real MedinTux schema with tables IndexNomPrenom (patient search index) and fchpat (patient details with FchPat_Nee birthdate field). Test data includes 20 synthetic French patient records inserted during setup plus 1 additional test patient 'TESTER' (born 2000-01-01). The verifier expects a demo output file at /home/ga/medintux_stored_procedures_demo.txt and tests the sp_search_patients procedure using the search term 'TESTER' (not 'a' as claimed in metadata).
